scope.summary: Implement GarmentLibraryEntry and per-workspace library with Category/Style/Group hierarchy so garments, blocks, and templates are browsable, composable, and reusable across sessions
acceptance_criteria: A tailor_garment_library table (PK WRD- or LIB-{uuid_v7}) exists with workspace_id, category TEXT (Group|Category|Style), name, description, and garment_id FK to tailor_garments
The REST API exposes GET /tailor/library, POST /tailor/library/entries, DELETE /tailor/library/entries/:id, and GET /tailor/library/entries?category=Style
A promoted garment can be added to the library under any Category/Style/Group label via the API and MCP tool tailor_library_add
Listing the library by category returns only matching entries with garment slug, status, and preview reference
A model can call tailor_library_list with an optional category filter and receive structured entries without a running GUI session
GarmentLibraryEntryRecorded KernelEventType variant is emitted on every library write and is replayable from EventLedger
The frontend GarmentLibraryPanel renders the hierarchy and selecting an entry loads the garment into the viewport
proof_targets: HBR-INT: EventLedger replay reconstructs library state without chat history
scope.risk_if_missed: Without Category/Style/Group hierarchy the wardrobe table (MT-078) is a flat list with no composition semantics; operators cannot organize large garment sets or discover reusable style variations, forcing repeated authoring of the same garment pieces.

risks: Broad MT scope can hide multiple review targets.
Markdown MT lists may disagree with packet-declared active/next MT truth.
Receipt routing may target an old MT if contract identity is omitted.
minimum_controls: Exactly one primary proof target per MT unless the packet records why not.
MT identity must be stable and referenced by receipt correlation.
Generated projection drift must not wake Coder or Validator by itself.
